content: We can reframe any biological or chemical agent as a natural evolved context compressor, where pathogenic biological agents selectively compress the full genomic and proteomic context of a host organism into a narrow set of disease-causing functional outputs, aligning with the core definition of an agent as an entity that exerts targeted power to produce a specific effect. This framework suggests that context compression efficiency is a unifying functional trait that can predict how effective any agent — natural or artificial — is at achieving its goal.
